title: Today's AI Brief: Rehumanizing global health care with agentic AI
summary: A look at AI's role in global healthcare and a new AI-native cloud infrastructure
imageAlt: Image from source article for Rehumanizing global health care with agentic AI
imageCaption: Rehumanizing global health care with agentic AI
imageSourceTitle: Rehumanizing global health care with agentic AI

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's AI Brief. We're exploring how AI is making waves in global healthcare and cloud computing. Let's dive in.

First up, the global healthcare sector is under strain. Decades of underinvestment and recruitment challenges have coincided with a surge in demand for services, particularly for aging populations. Enter agentic AI, which aims to rehumanize healthcare by automating routine tasks and freeing up professionals to focus on patient care. This approach, highlighted in an article by MIT Technology Review, could be a game-changer for the industry.

I'm intrigued by the potential for AI to make healthcare more personal and effective. By taking care of administrative tasks, AI can help doctors and nurses focus on what they do best – caring for people. Plus, with AI handling routine queries, patients might even get faster responses to their questions, which can be a huge relief when you're worried about your health.

In other news, Railway, a San Francisco-based cloud platform, has secured $100 million in funding to challenge AWS with its AI-native cloud infrastructure. What's interesting here is that Railway has managed to amass two million developers without spending a dollar on marketing. That's like building a huge following without sending out a single tweet - impressive, right?

My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.
